atomic_effects.definition: Read semantic state from one or more named domains.
atomic_effects.observable_behavior: The value read constrains reordering with aliasing writes.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Assume every registered domain is read when a summary is absent.

atomic_effects.definition: Change semantic state in one or more named domains.
atomic_effects.observable_behavior: Aliasing reads and guards can observe the change.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Assume every registered domain is written when a summary is absent.

atomic_effects.definition: Allocate engine-managed storage or a value container.
atomic_effects.observable_behavior: Allocation may affect GC metadata and may fail through engine control flow.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Treat allocation as a heap and GC write with a safepoint.

atomic_effects.definition: Create, replace, or propagate the active exception.
atomic_effects.observable_behavior: Control transfers to exception handling after ordered cleanup.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Assume an operation may throw unless a totality proof excludes it.

atomic_effects.observable_behavior: The current continuation has no normal return.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Treat possible bailout as a terminal control-flow edge.

atomic_effects.definition: Invoke an internal function or handler.
atomic_effects.observable_behavior: The callee can mutate engine state or delegate to PHP.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Use the fail-closed summary until a validated callee summary exists.

atomic_effects.definition: Invoke PHP code with a Zend-compatible call frame.
atomic_effects.observable_behavior: Arbitrary user-visible effects, throws, and frame observation may occur.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Assume reentry, throw, observation, and aliasing reads and writes.

atomic_effects.definition: Enter PHP while another engine action remains active.
atomic_effects.observable_behavior: User code can inspect frames and mutate aliases before the outer action completes.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Assume arbitrary PHP behavior and require materialization.

atomic_effects.definition: Run value or object destruction logic.
atomic_effects.observable_behavior: Destruction can execute PHP, throw, observe frames, and trigger GC.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Assume full destructor closure unless exact type proof excludes it.

atomic_effects.definition: Expose interpreter-visible frame state to an observer or user code.
atomic_effects.observable_behavior: Arguments, locals, temporaries, and call-chain position become observable.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Materialize the full visible frame before observation.

atomic_effects.observable_behavior: Callbacks or termination can occur at this ordering point.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Treat the boundary as frame-observing and control-flow changing.

atomic_effects.observable_behavior: Live frame and ownership state persist until resumption.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Materialize all live state and forbid motion across suspension.

atomic_effects.definition: Resume a suspended generator or fiber.
atomic_effects.observable_behavior: Previously materialized state becomes active under current engine state.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Reload and revalidate all cross-suspension facts.

atomic_effects.definition: Read or change state outside engine-managed memory.
atomic_effects.observable_behavior: Other processes or subsequent operations can observe ordering.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Preserve program order around external state.

atomic_effects.definition: End the active program or execution context.
atomic_effects.conservative_default: Treat possible termination as a terminal edge.

memory_domains.definition: Arguments in the active Zend-compatible frame.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Calls and observers can inspect argument values and ownership.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Assume arguments may alias references and heap zvals.

memory_domains.definition: Compiled variables and local slots in the active frame.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Symbol tables, references, and observers can expose local values.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Assume locals may be indirectly visible.

memory_domains.definition: Temporary values with live ownership obligations.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Cleanup and exception paths observe their lifetime ordering.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Keep every live temporary materializable at a boundary.

memory_domains.definition: Active calls, oplines, and return-continuation state.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Backtraces, errors, observers, and exceptions inspect it.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Materialize a Zend-compatible call chain at every boundary.

memory_domains.definition: Runtime symbol-table bindings and indirect slots.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Dynamic access can connect names to locals and references.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Assume PHP calls and reentry may read and write it.

memory_domains.definition: Mutable runtime lookup and dispatch caches.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Calls and table changes can replace cached targets.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Invalidate cache facts across calls, reentry, and table writes.

memory_domains.definition: Generic zval contents, type tags, and refcounted payload links.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Copies, moves, assignments, and destruction change value semantics.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Assume aliasing through references and containers.

memory_domains.definition: Array buckets, keys, packed/hash layout, and element values.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Mutation and COW separation change layout and aliases.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Assume array writes invalidate layout and key facts.

memory_domains.definition: Object identity, properties, handlers, and destructor state.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Handlers and destructors can execute PHP and mutate properties.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Assume object access can invoke user-visible behavior.

memory_domains.definition: Refcounted string contents and identity.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Lifetime and identity can affect aliases and cleanup.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Assume shared ownership unless uniqueness is proven.

memory_domains.definition: Reference containers and their bound zvals.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Writes through a reference change every aliasing view.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Assume reference pointees alias heap and frame zvals.

memory_domains.definition: Refcount, possible-root, and cycle-collection metadata.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Refcount changes can schedule collection and destruction.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Treat releases and allocations as GC-visible writes.

memory_domains.definition: Active and previous exception state.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Throwing, cleanup, and handlers change control flow and ordering.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Assume calls, destructors, and magic handlers may change it.

memory_domains.definition: Observer callback and notification state.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Observers can inspect calls and frames.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Assume configured observers require full frame materialization.

memory_domains.definition: Interrupt flags and dispatched interrupt state.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Interrupts can invoke callbacks or terminate execution.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Treat every interrupt poll as an observable boundary.

memory_domains.definition: Runtime class definitions and resolution state.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Autoloading and reentry can affect class resolution.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Invalidate class facts across calls and reentry.

memory_domains.definition: Runtime function definitions and resolution state.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Calls and reentry can affect target resolution.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Invalidate function facts across calls and reentry.

memory_domains.definition: Generator suspension, yielded value, and saved frame state.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Yield and resume transfer live ownership across time.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Materialize and revalidate all live generator state.

memory_domains.definition: Fiber suspension and saved execution state.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: Fiber switching changes which materialized frame is active.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Treat switching as a full suspension barrier.

memory_domains.definition: State outside engine-managed memory.
memory_domains.observable_behavior: I/O and termination are visible beyond the current request.
memory_domains.conservative_default: Preserve external ordering unless independence is proven.

alias_relations.reason: Arguments contain zvals and may be passed by reference.
Symbol-table exposure creates indirect local access.
A local zval can hold a reference container.
Temporary values carry zval payloads and cleanup obligations.
A zval can wrap or unwrap a reference.
A reference may bind an array-containing zval.
A reference may bind an object-containing zval.
Array buckets contain zvals, including references.
Object properties contain zvals and handler-visible state.
Refcount and possible-root metadata govern zval payload lifetime.

ownership_states.definition: A slot has no live semantic value.
ownership_states.observable_behavior: It carries no cleanup and cannot be read.
ownership_states.conservative_default: Reject reads or transfers from this state.

ownership_states.definition: A live value is usable without owning a release.
ownership_states.observable_behavior: The lender must outlive every use.
ownership_states.conservative_default: Reject escape or cleanup without an explicit ownership conversion.

ownership_states.definition: A live value owns exactly one release obligation.
ownership_states.observable_behavior: Every exit must transfer or perform that release once.
ownership_states.conservative_default: Retain the obligation on all control-flow edges.

ownership_states.definition: An owned value shares refcounted storage.
ownership_states.observable_behavior: Mutation requires visible separation or compatible shared mutation.

ownership_states.definition: The source ownership obligation was transferred.
ownership_states.observable_behavior: The source cannot be used or cleaned again.

ownership_states.definition: The source release obligation was conditionally consumed.
ownership_states.observable_behavior: The source cannot be used or released again.
Its payload lifetime ended after ordered destruction.

ownership_actions.definition: Create a non-owning view of a live value.
ownership_actions.observable_behavior: No refcount changes and the source remains live.
ownership_actions.conservative_default: Reject if lender lifetime is not proven.

ownership_actions.definition: Copy a zval and add a new owning reference.
ownership_actions.conservative_default: Model the addref as a write and produce shared ownership.

ownership_actions.definition: Transfer one cleanup obligation from source to result.
ownership_actions.observable_behavior: The source becomes terminal and the result becomes owned.
ownership_actions.conservative_default: Reject unless the source is uniquely owned and live.

ownership_actions.definition: Initialize a slot with a new owned value.
ownership_actions.observable_behavior: The new value creates exactly one cleanup obligation.

ownership_actions.definition: Initialize a slot with a borrowed value.
ownership_actions.observable_behavior: The new value has no independent cleanup obligation.

ownership_actions.definition: Transfer an owned value into a destination slot or call argument.
ownership_actions.observable_behavior: Exactly one obligation moves and the source becomes terminal.
ownership_actions.conservative_default: Reject implicit copies and require edge-complete transfer.

ownership_actions.definition: Consume the final uniquely owned value and run required destruction.
ownership_actions.observable_behavior: Destructor, reentry, exception, observation, and GC behavior may occur in order.
ownership_actions.conservative_default: Use conditional_destroy unless final ownership is proven, then apply full destructor closure.

ownership_actions.definition: Release one owner and destroy the payload if it was final.
ownership_actions.observable_behavior: Refcount changes can trigger ordered destruction or GC.
ownership_actions.conservative_default: Assume the final-owner path and full destructor closure.

ownership_actions.definition: Create unique array storage before mutation of shared storage.
ownership_actions.observable_behavior: Allocation, refcounts, buckets, and aliases visibly change.
ownership_actions.conservative_default: Require separation for shared arrays before write.

ownership_actions.observable_behavior: Semantic value and cleanup obligation remain identical.
ownership_actions.conservative_default: Pair with an explicit ownership action if any obligation changes.

predicates.definition: The operation may perform final release or destruction.
predicates.observable_behavior: PHP reentry, throw, frame observation, and GC may follow.
predicates.conservative_default: Assume true without an exact non-destructing-type proof.

predicates.definition: The operation may take a non-local bailout.
predicates.observable_behavior: No normal continuation follows that edge.
predicates.conservative_default: Assume true for an unmodeled non-local engine action.

predicates.definition: The operation may execute PHP before completing.
predicates.observable_behavior: PHP can mutate aliases, throw, and inspect frames.
predicates.conservative_default: Assume true for calls, handlers, and destructors.

predicates.definition: Object access may invoke a magic handler.
predicates.observable_behavior: User PHP and exception behavior may replace a plain memory access.
predicates.conservative_default: Assume true without exact handler proof.

predicates.definition: The operation may expose frame state.
predicates.observable_behavior: Unmaterialized values and ordering become externally visible.
predicates.conservative_default: Assume true at calls and callbacks.

predicates.definition: An interrupt may be dispatched at the operation.
predicates.observable_behavior: Callback or termination changes control and visible ordering.
predicates.conservative_default: Assume true at an interrupt boundary.

predicates.definition: The operation may suspend the current execution context.
predicates.observable_behavior: Live state persists until execution resumes.
predicates.conservative_default: Assume true for generator and fiber switching.

barriers.definition: A point requiring recoverable interpreter-visible state.
barriers.observable_behavior: Runtime services can require materialized values and control state.

barriers.definition: A boundary at which nested PHP execution may begin.
barriers.observable_behavior: Nested execution can inspect frames and mutate engine and heap state.
barriers.conservative_default: Materialize all visible state and invalidate call-sensitive guards.

barriers.definition: A boundary at which ordered destruction may run.
barriers.observable_behavior: Cleanup may reenter PHP, throw, observe frames, and mutate aliases.
barriers.conservative_default: Apply destructor closure and preserve cleanup order.

barriers.definition: A boundary separating normal and exceptional continuations.
barriers.observable_behavior: Pending exception and ordered cleanup determine the next handler.
barriers.conservative_default: Preserve exception state and source cleanup order.

barriers.definition: A boundary to a non-local terminal continuation.
barriers.observable_behavior: Normal-return work must not run on this edge.
barriers.conservative_default: Preserve materialized state and terminate the local continuation.

barriers.definition: A boundary at which configured observers inspect execution.
barriers.observable_behavior: Frame values and call ordering are visible.
barriers.conservative_default: Materialize the visible frame and preserve call ordering.

barriers.definition: A boundary at which interruption may be dispatched.
barriers.observable_behavior: Callback, exception, or termination can occur.
barriers.conservative_default: Materialize the frame and preserve external ordering.

barriers.definition: A boundary across suspension and subsequent resumption.
barriers.observable_behavior: Live values, ownership, and frame state persist across time.
barriers.conservative_default: Materialize all live state and revalidate every guard on resume.

guard_facts.definition: A value has a guarded semantic type.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: Type-specialized operations are valid only while the fact holds.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Recheck after alias writes, calls, or reference changes.
guard_facts.required_proof: A dominating type guard plus no invalidating path.

guard_facts.definition: An object has an exact guarded class identity.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: Handler and property specialization depends on the identity.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Recheck after calls, magic handlers, or alias writes.
guard_facts.required_proof: An exact-class guard and stable object identity.

guard_facts.definition: A refcounted payload has one owner and no writable aliases.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: In-place mutation is legal only while uniqueness holds.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Lose uniqueness on copy, alias write, call, reentry, or destruction.
guard_facts.required_proof: Observed count one plus complete alias and escape proof.

guard_facts.definition: An array currently uses packed layout.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: Indexed access and bounds specialization depend on layout.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Invalidate on array/reference writes, COW, calls, or destruction.
guard_facts.required_proof: A packed-layout guard plus alias-complete mutation proof.

guard_facts.definition: A guarded array key is present or absent as recorded.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: Element specialization depends on bucket membership.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Invalidate on aliasing array/reference writes or calls.
guard_facts.required_proof: A key lookup guard plus alias-complete mutation proof.

guard_facts.definition: A reference container remains bound to the guarded pointee.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: Alias and value facts depend on binding identity.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Invalidate on slot/reference writes and calls.
guard_facts.required_proof: A binding-identity guard and complete alias proof.

guard_facts.definition: A runtime cache slot contains the guarded target.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: Fast dispatch and lookup depend on cache contents.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Invalidate on cache/table writes, calls, and reentry.
guard_facts.required_proof: A cache generation and target identity guard.

guard_facts.definition: A function lookup resolves to the guarded implementation.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: Call dispatch depends on the function-table entry.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Invalidate on function/cache writes, calls, or reentry.
guard_facts.required_proof: An identity guard over function-table and cache state.

guard_facts.definition: A class lookup resolves to the guarded class entry.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: Object construction and handler choice depend on it.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Invalidate on class/cache writes, calls, or reentry.
guard_facts.required_proof: An identity guard over class-table and cache state.

guard_facts.definition: Engine exception state is clear at the guarded point.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: Normal continuation is valid only while no exception is pending.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Invalidate on any possibly throwing or reentrant action.
guard_facts.required_proof: An exception-state check plus a no-throw path proof.

guard_facts.definition: A materialized frame slot retains its guarded value.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: Observers and deoptimization depend on exact visible state.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Invalidate on frame writes or observation-capable boundaries.
guard_facts.required_proof: A slot version guard and synchronized materialization state.

guard_facts.definition: Releasing a guarded value cannot run user-visible destruction.
guard_facts.observable_behavior: Cleanup motion is valid only while this fact holds.
guard_facts.conservative_default: Assume destruction is possible unless exact type and lifetime prove otherwise.
guard_facts.required_proof: An exact non-destructing type and complete alias/lifetime proof.

composition_rules.definition: Possible destruction closes over all destructor-visible behavior.
composition_rules.observable_behavior: PHP reentry, throw, frame observation, and ordered mutation are retained.
composition_rules.conservative_default: Apply the complete closure whenever destruction cannot be excluded.

composition_rules.definition: A possible bailout has a distinct terminal edge.
composition_rules.observable_behavior: Normal success work never executes on the bailout edge.
composition_rules.conservative_default: Construct the terminal edge before optimization.

composition_rules.definition: A PHP call has reentrant, throwing, observing behavior.
composition_rules.observable_behavior: Frames and aliases are visible to arbitrary user code.

composition_rules.definition: An internal call without a validated summary uses the global fail-closed policy.
composition_rules.observable_behavior: No purity, motion, or publication assumption is permitted.
composition_rules.conservative_default: Read and write all domains, apply all effects and barriers, and reject publication.

composition_rules.definition: Possible magic object access closes over PHP call behavior.
composition_rules.observable_behavior: A property operation can execute PHP, mutate state, and throw.
composition_rules.conservative_default: Apply PHP call closure without exact handler proof.

composition_rules.definition: Possible frame observation requires a visible compatible frame.
composition_rules.observable_behavior: Observer callbacks see exact values and call position.

composition_rules.definition: Possible interruption requires recoverable visible state.
composition_rules.observable_behavior: Dispatch can observe the frame or terminate.
composition_rules.conservative_default: Materialize the frame and preserve the interrupt edge.

composition_rules.definition: Possible suspension requires persistent frame and ownership state.
composition_rules.observable_behavior: Resume must reconstruct the same live semantics.
composition_rules.conservative_default: Materialize live values and invalidate cross-suspension guards.

composition_rules.definition: A write invalidates guards over every may-alias domain.
composition_rules.observable_behavior: Stale type, layout, binding, count, and cache facts cannot be reused.
composition_rules.conservative_default: Assume domains may alias unless disjointness is proven.

composition_rules.definition: Throwing preserves source-order cleanup before handler transfer.
composition_rules.observable_behavior: Destructors and pending exception state remain observable in order.
composition_rules.conservative_default: Do not reorder cleanup across a possible throw.

composition_rules.definition: COW separation is an ownership, allocation, and memory effect.
composition_rules.observable_behavior: Refcounts, storage identity, aliases, and layout change before mutation.
composition_rules.conservative_default: Never erase or move separation without uniqueness and alias proof.
